examples.name: aerogram
examples.title: Aerogram
examples.description: Dispatches on maps and cartography, from dead projections to field surveys
examples.brief: A maps-and-cartography letter dressed as airmail stationery: a card grid of issues on paper-blue-tinted white, flipping to a chart-room slate in dark mode via the theme toggle. The first screen shows the masthead with a compass-rose SVG mark, a static subscribe form styled as an address label, and the latest issue cards.
examples.signature: issue cards are edged with a CSS airmail chevron border in alternating accent and muted stripes

content.theme: dispatches on maps and cartography: projections, forgotten atlases, and field surveying

examples.name: agate
examples.title: Agate
examples.description: The complete language reference for a whitespace-strict template language
examples.brief: A calm, bookish language reference set in IBM Plex: a single reading column, teal links, and chapter numbers in the margin. The homepage first screen presents the language name, a one-paragraph credo about strict whitespace, and a numbered table of chapters ruled with hairlines. Everything defers to the measure and rhythm of the text.
examples.signature: each chapter opener floats an oversized watermark of the delimiters {% %} behind the heading at low opacity

content.theme: reference for Agate, a whitespace-strict template language that compiles templates to native render functions

examples.name: albedo
examples.title: Albedo
examples.description: Flag design studies for imagined polar research micronations, drafted in pure SVG
examples.brief: A vexillology studio's portfolio of flags designed for imagined polar research micronations: crisp geometric fields, restrained charge symbols, everything drafted as exact-ratio SVG. Snow-white page, cool grey chrome, and a single glacial teal accent let the flag colors do the talking. The homepage first screen presents a disciplined card grid of flags flying at consistent hoist, each card annotated like a drafting sheet.
examples.signature: every flag card carries its exact aspect ratio (2:3, 1:2, 5:8) as a small drafting annotation in the corner

content.theme: flag design studies for imagined polar research micronations

examples.name: alder
examples.title: Alder
examples.description: A home-lab wiki documenting one basement rack: nodes, switches, and watts
examples.brief: A home-lab hardware wiki styled as operator documentation: near-black green-tinted background, monospace headings, and dotted borders that read like ASCII rules. The homepage first screen shows the rack name as a shell prompt, a short uptime-style status line, and the sidebar inventory tree of nodes and switches. Config snippets and service files appear as fenced code blocks throughout.
examples.signature: every hardware page header renders a spec strip — CPU / RAM / idle watts — formatted as prompt output with a $ prefix

content.theme: documentation for a basement home-lab rack: compute nodes, network switches, and the power budget

examples.name: alizarin
examples.title: Alizarin

examples.brief: A stage lighting designer for dance and opera whose portfolio reads like a marked-up light plot: blackout ground, red gel accents, condensed Big Shoulders headlines at poster scale. The layout is a deliberate collage — rotated production title blocks, raw borders, taped-on labels. The first screen shows the designer's name in huge condensed caps with production blocks scattered beneath, each overlapped by labeled SVG gel swatches like samples taped to a plot.
examples.signature: labeled SVG gel-swatch rectangles overlap the rotated production blocks like samples taped to a light plot

content.theme: stage lighting designs for dance and opera productions, documented cue by cue

examples.name: ampere
examples.title: Ampere
examples.description: A modular home battery that stacks like shelving and installs in a day
examples.brief: A home-battery company with appliance-grade calm: cool blue-grey neutrals, Inter Tight headlines, hairline dividers, numbers doing most of the talking. Full-bleed bands alternate between the product story and specification callouts, each band a quiet tonal shift. First screen: the headline stat '87% of your evening, off the grid' beside a tall SVG rendering of the stacked battery unit.
examples.signature: an SVG battery cell in the hero is filled to the exact percentage quoted in the headline stat

content.theme: a modular stacking home battery for evening self-sufficiency

examples.name: annal
examples.title: Annal
examples.description: Version chronicle for a local-first note-taking app with backlink graphs
examples.brief: A bookish, ledger-like chronicle for a local-first note-taking app. Warm paper ground, rust accent, Newsreader headings with oldstyle figures, and hairline red ledger rules separating releases. examples.signature: each release opens with a red ledger-rule and its semver set in oldstyle figures hanging in the left margin

content.theme: release notes for a local-first markdown note-taking app with backlink graphs

examples.name: anthracite
examples.title: Anthracite
examples.description: Static analysis for shell scripts, documented rule by rule in a coal-dark terminal
examples.brief: Documentation for a shell-script static analyzer, framed entirely as a terminal session: coal-black chrome, amber accents, mono type throughout. The homepage first screen shows the framed window with a prompt line running `anthracite lint deploy.sh` and a findings list styled as annotated diagnostics. Interior rule pages keep the frame and read as calm, dense reference cards.
examples.signature: the nav lists every lint rule by its code (AN101, AN214) in amber mono before the rule title

content.theme: documentation for Anthracite, a static analyzer for shell scripts that flags unsafe expansions, quoting bugs, and portability traps

examples.name: apatite
examples.title: Apatite
examples.description: Property-based testing with automatic shrinking, explained by example
examples.brief: Testing-framework docs that make property-based testing feel approachable: bright white ground, sky-blue accent, round Outfit headings, generous soft-cornered callouts. examples.signature: failing examples are annotated with a shrink trace: a row of progressively smaller mono chips ending at the minimal counterexample

content.theme: a property-based testing framework with integrated shrinking and replayable seeds

examples.name: aphelion
examples.title: Aphelion
examples.description: A winter festival of slow cinema and long takes, screened far from the sun
examples.brief: A film festival site with the hush of a dark auditorium: near-black warm gray, ivory serif display type, a single projector-red accent used sparingly. The homepage splits asymmetrically — the left half holds the festival name in enormous serif italic over the dates (29 January – 1 February 2026, Bergen), the right half scrolls the screening lineup as a spare list of titles, directors, and times. Restraint everywhere; the type is the marquee.
examples.signature: every film in the lineup opens with a 2.39:1 letterboxed SVG frame holding a unique abstract duotone still built from two palette tones

content.theme: a fictional slow-cinema festival programming durational films about winter light, ferries, and waiting

examples.name: apogee
examples.title: Apogee
examples.description: A three-person design studio for aerospace and climate-tech companies
examples.brief: A design agency site with the confidence of a flight manual: warm off-white paper, a single vivid ultramarine accent, Syne display type at hero scale. The split hero puts a terse studio statement on the left and a thin-stroke SVG orbital diagram on the right, annotated like an engineering drawing. Everything else is quiet — hairline rules, generous margins, numbered lists.
examples.signature: case studies list as numbered flight-log rows, each carrying a small right-aligned altitude figure in the margin

content.theme: a boutique design studio working exclusively with aerospace and climate-tech startups

examples.name: apple
examples.title: Apple
examples.description: A pomological manual on heirloom apple varieties, cider-making techniques, and heritage orchard husbandry
examples.brief: A pomological monograph presented as an heirloom agricultural guide: cream laid-paper background, deep pine-black text, a rich orchard-green accent, and a classic two-page spread layout with running headers and folio numbers. The homepage first screen features a clean title band with a delicate hand-drawn SVG apple leaf mark, a central introductory dedication paragraph, and the numbered list of monograph chapters. In dark mode, the palette transitions to night-orchard charcoal and sage tones with a desaturated apple-green accent. Inside, chapters read sequentially using next/prev navigation anchors at the bottom of the page.
examples.signature: navigation links and chapter headers are accompanied by a hand-drawn SVG apple blossom icon that animates from outline to filled bloom using a CSS stroke-dashoffset transition upon hover

content.theme: a pomological manual on heirloom apple varieties, cider-making techniques, and heritage orchard husbandry

examples.name: ascent
examples.title: Ascent
examples.description: An independent foundry reviving lettering from mid-century shop signage
examples.brief: A font foundry storefront that treats type as the only imagery: gallery-white ground, deep violet accent, Libre Caslon Text display over Work Sans captions. A restrained header band introduces the foundry, then a uniform card grid lists the catalog, each card pairing a typeface name with oversized SVG letterform art clipped by the card edge. First screen: the foundry statement above the first row of specimen cards.
examples.signature: the hero sets a giant Aa cropped by the viewport edge, annotated with labeled baseline, x-height, and ascender metric lines

content.theme: an independent font foundry selling digital revivals of hand-painted mid-century shop signage

examples.name: aurora
examples.title: Aurora
examples.description: Restoration plates and repair notes for vintage fountain pens
examples.brief: A fountain pen restoration journal presented like a museum catalog after hours: ink-dark navy pages, DM Serif Display headings, and a fountain-pen-blue accent. Restoration write-ups stack in a masonry wall of plates, each topped by its catalog label. The first screen shows the masthead with a nib SVG mark, a line about patience and shellac, and the first plates of the collection.
examples.signature: every post is topped by a numbered museum-catalog label in small caps, styled like 'Plate No. 07'

content.theme: restoring vintage fountain pens, from sac replacement to nib smoothing

examples.name: bada
examples.title: Bada
examples.description: Cold-water swim logs and quiet essays from a northern sea coast
examples.brief: A cold-water swimming journal named for the Korean word for sea. The homepage opens with the site name small at top, the latest swim's title at hero scale, and its water-temperature badge floating beside the date.
examples.signature: each swim's water temperature renders as a small circular badge tinted along a cold-to-mild blue ramp

examples.name: banana
examples.title: Banana
examples.description: A playful, organic wiki dedicated to the botany, cultivation, cooking, and global folklore of the banana plant.
examples.brief: A playful, sunlit wiki for banana cultivation and botany. The homepage layout uses a bento-grid layout of unequal card tiles. The bands of the homepage in order are: (1) A header band with a playful hand-drawn logo, title, and a simple search bar; (2) A main bento-grid band containing a large featured article card with a golden background, a smaller 'Did You Know?' folklore card, a grid of categories (Botanical, Culinary, Cultivation), and a quick-links card listing recent updates; (3) A footer band with site credits and a theme toggle. Light mode uses warm cream and yellow tones with a deep banana-gold accent; dark mode shifts to a rich dark charcoal-brown with a bright golden-yellow accent.
examples.signature: each wiki article page includes a right-aligned vertical specs rail bordered by a thin dashed line, containing cultivation details (like humidity and soil pH) housed in warm yellow pill badges that rotate slightly (+/-2deg) on hover

content.theme: botany, cultivation, culinary history, and global folklore of the banana plant

examples.name: baobab
examples.title: Baobab

examples.signature: each patch page embeds an SVG signal-flow diagram with modules as outlined blocks and patch cables as curved accent strokes

content.theme: an encyclopedia of patches for the fictional Karst-6 modular synthesizer, from drones to sequenced basses

examples.name: baram
examples.title: Baram

examples.signature: each timeline entry carries a small inline SVG wind arrow rotated to the recorded wind direction from front matter

examples.name: basalt
examples.title: Basalt

examples.name: beryl
examples.title: Beryl

examples.title: Bolide

Scanline textures and chromatic text shadows are allowed; taste comes from committing hard, not from polish.

examples.name: bora
examples.title: Bora

examples.signature: section headings sit on a thin double rule like a marking-gauge scribe line running the full content width

examples.name: breccia
examples.title: Breccia

examples.name: brume
examples.title: Brume

examples.name: buckram
examples.title: Buckram

examples.signature: ration tables render as ruled ledger rows with dotted leaders running from item to gram count

examples.name: bulletin
examples.title: The Bulletin

examples.name: burin
examples.title: Burin

examples.name: cachet
examples.title: Cachet

examples.name: cadence
examples.title: Cadence

examples.brief: A light, grounded changelog for open split-keyboard firmware. examples.title: Chinook

examples.name: cirrus

examples.name: coptic

examples.name: corundum

examples.name: courant

examples.description: Feature flags with percentage rollouts, typed SDKs, and kill switches, clearly documented
examples.brief: Bright, geometric docs for a feature-flag service: near-white paper, crisp violet accent, rounded rectangles that echo toggle switches. The homepage first screen pairs a bold Sora headline with a two-column grid of rollout guides, each card carrying a small on/off pill. Friendly without being cartoonish — the geometry does the charm.
examples.signature: the sidebar marks the current page with a toggle pill flipped to its on state instead of a highlight bar

content.theme: docs for Mica, a feature-flag service with percentage rollouts, typed flag SDKs, and instant kill switches

examples.name: milepost
examples.title: Milepost
examples.description: Server release notes and status for a voxel sandbox with a plugin API
examples.brief: A friendly roadside-themed release log for a voxel sandbox game server, shipping light and dark with a toggle. Warm cream ground by day, deep night-road ground by dark, signpost-orange accent, rounded milestone plates marching down a center spine. First screen: the wordmark on a road-sign plate and the two most recent milestone releases below it.
examples.signature: the timeline is drawn as a roadside distance-marker strip — each release is a milestone post with the semver on its plate

content.theme: release notes and server status for a voxel sandbox game server with a plugin API

examples.name: missive
examples.title: Missive
examples.description: A letter on materials science: alloys, ceramics, and the labs that break them
examples.brief: A materials-science letter styled like a specimen archive: charcoal ground, precise grid, amber accent used like a heat-treat glow. Issue cards sit in a uniform grid under a stark geometric masthead and a one-line static subscribe bar. The first screen shows the masthead, the subscribe bar, and the top two rows of specimen-labeled issue cards.
examples.signature: issue cards open with a specimen-label header: material name, batch number, and a small color chip

content.theme: a letter on materials science: alloys, ceramics, and the labs that test them to failure

examples.name: mistral
examples.title: Mistral
examples.description: Ride reports from long-distance cycling into the headwinds of Provence
examples.brief: A long-distance cycling journal with a confident editorial voice. Warm off-white pages, magenta accent used sparingly on links and stats, Newsreader headlines with real presence. The homepage hero splits asymmetrically: the latest ride's title and standfirst on the left, an oversized distance-and-elevation stat pair stacked on the right, with the ride archive listed beneath.
examples.signature: each ride report's distance and elevation gain render as an oversized two-line stat pair in the hero's right half

content.theme: ride reports from long-distance cycling through Provence headwinds

examples.name: mizzle
examples.title: Mizzle
examples.description: A slow journal of tea ceremony practice, utensils, and seasonal sweets
examples.brief: A tea ceremony practice journal, quiet as a tatami room: soft green-white paper, Cormorant Garamond at generous sizes, and one matcha-green accent. The first screen shows the masthead at top, the hanging-scroll rule descending through it, and the latest session's title and season note aligned to the rule. Entries record temae practiced, utensils used, and one lesson learned.
examples.signature: a single vertical rule descends from the masthead down the full page like a hanging scroll, entries aligned to it

content.theme: practice notes from learning Japanese tea ceremony over a year of lessons

examples.name: moire
examples.title: Moiré
examples.description: An interference-pattern library of overlapping line fields and tessellations
examples.brief: An op-art pattern library where every piece is two or three overlapping SVG line fields producing live interference: rotated grids, concentric rings, offset hatching. Near-black green-tinted ground with chartreuse accents makes the white line work vibrate. The homepage first screen pairs a shimmering hero interference field with the top rows of a masonry wall of pattern tiles, each labeled with its line pitch and rotation angle.
examples.signature: two overlapping SVG line fields in the hero drift at slightly different angles to produce a live moiré shimmer, stilled under reduced motion

content.theme: an interference-pattern library of overlapping line fields and tessellations

examples.name: momentum
examples.title: Momentum
examples.description: A local-first notes app that files thoughts by momentum, not by folders
examples.brief: A calm note-taking app landing: paper-white ground with the faintest green cast, deep teal accent, Instrument Sans throughout with a soft, even rhythm. The split hero pairs a short thesis about frictionless capture on the left with a visual of the app's note stack on the right. Rounded corners stay subtle at 6px; the whole site should feel like an uncluttered desk.
examples.signature: the hero visual is a pile of note cards each rotated one to three degrees, stacked with real page titles peeking out

content.theme: a local-first note-taking app that resurfaces notes by recency of thought instead of folder trees

examples.name: monsoon
examples.title: Monsoon
examples.description: A fermentation journal of misos, krauts, and patient long brews
examples.brief: A home-fermentation journal that feels like a cool dark pantry. Deep green-black palette with a living pale-green accent, Vollkorn headings, and soft rounded surfaces like crocks on a shelf. The homepage hero splits between an introduction to the current batch on the left and a live-looking list of active ferments with day counts on the right.
examples.signature: each ferment entry displays an oversized 'Day N' counter computed from its start date in front matter

content.theme: a fermentation journal of misos, krauts, and long-brew projects

examples.name: moonbow
examples.title: Moonbow
examples.description: Patch notes and listening logs from a generative music practice
examples.brief: A generative music blog in midnight indigo with Sora's precise geometry and a soft violet accent like a synth LED. Uniform cards each open with their sequencer-grid motif; code blocks show the actual patch logic. The first screen holds a header with the Ctrl+K hint, a one-line thesis about systems that compose themselves, and the top row of patch cards.
examples.signature: a step-sequencer grid of accent squares, deterministically seeded from the post slug, heads each entry

content.theme: building generative music systems with probability tables and slow LFOs

examples.name: murmur
examples.title: Murmur
examples.description: Dispatches from the hadal zone — a deep-sea science podcast
examples.brief: A deep-sea science podcast in abyssal blues. Split hero: the show title floating over layered SVG wave-line art on the left, the latest expedition episode with its depth gauge on the right. Dark, quiet, bioluminescent-blue accent; interior pages stay spare and instrument-like, with duration chips styled as sonar readouts.
examples.signature: every episode carries a vertical depth-gauge scale marking the dive depth the episode reports from, ruled like an instrument face

content.theme: a deep-sea science podcast reporting from hadal-zone research expeditions

examples.name: nabi
examples.title: Nabi
examples.description: A playful food quarterly chasing Korean market seasons, one banchan at a time
examples.brief: A warm, mischievous food quarterly named for the Korean word for butterfly. Cream pages, raspberry accent, generous serif display with bouncy oversized pull-quotes; each feature opens with a small-caps kicker (MARKET DAY / FERMENT), a dek from the description, and a named writer's byline. The homepage first screen is the Nabi nameplate with a fluttering-butterfly SVG mark above a lead feature and a two-column well of seasonal stories.
examples.signature: a small butterfly SVG marks each story's kicker and tilts its wings on hover

content.theme: Korean seasonal cooking and open-air market culture

examples.name: newton
examples.title: Newton
examples.description: Cold-hardy fruit trees and berry stock, grafted and grown on site
examples.brief: A plant nursery landing with seed-catalog sincerity: green-tinted paper ground, deep leaf-green accent, Lora headlines and roomy line-height throughout. A header band introduces the nursery, then a uniform card grid of flagship stock — apples, currants, haskaps — each card with a botanical SVG line drawing. First screen shows the band plus the first row of cards.
examples.signature: every plant card carries a circular hardiness-zone stamp in its top corner, rendered like an inked rubber stamp

content.theme: a cold-hardy fruit tree and berry nursery

examples.name: newyork
examples.title: Newyork
examples.description: A weekly newsletter covering New York architecture, hidden infrastructure, and street-level urban design history.
examples.brief: A weekly cultural dispatch celebrating New York’s street-level history and urban design. Set in a broadsheet newspaper aesthetic with column rules, justified text, and heavy black dividers. The bands of the homepage in order are: (1) A masthead band featuring a large SVG typographic 'NEWYORK' logo, the issue number, a date, and a static subscribe form styled as a newspaper classified ad; (2) A main broadsheet story grid band containing a featured lead article on the left with a large typographic headline, and a right-hand sidebar showing a vertical list of previous issues with dates; (3) A footer band containing links to standalone pages, a copyright notice, and a theme toggle. Light mode uses limestone-tinted white with a dark brick-red accent; dark mode shifts to a soot-black background with a desaturated terracotta accent.
examples.signature: the homepage masthead features a stylized SVG typographic wordmark of 'NEWYORK' where the letter 'O' is drawn as a classic circular transit token that spins 360 degrees on hover using a CSS transform

content.theme: New York history, architecture, street layout, hidden infrastructure, and neighborhood design evolution

examples.name: nil
examples.title: Nil
examples.description: A newsletter on minimalism, void, silence, and the mathematical concept of zero
examples.brief: An ultra-minimalist newsletter exploring the void, negative space, and absolute zero in design and science. The layout is a stark centered column with extreme whitespace, utilizing a cool light-gray background that shifts to deep slate-gray in dark mode. The homepage consists of three distinct vertical bands: first, a quiet masthead with the title 'Nil' and an empty SVG circle representing zero; second, a single-column list of issues starting at issue #00, where each post is framed as a text card with the empty set signature slash; third, a colophon footer with a simple email signup field and minimal text links.
examples.signature: each issue teaser card features a faint diagonal accent-colored line running from top-left to bottom-right (mimicking the mathematical empty set symbol Ø), which transitions to horizontal and fades to 10% opacity when the card is hovered

content.theme: philosophical, artistic, and scientific meditations on zero, negative space, and the concept of the void

examples.name: nimbus
examples.title: Nimbus
examples.description: Plain-English weather briefings for private pilots, one METAR at a time
examples.brief: A meteorology blog for student and private pilots, built with crisp geometric type and chart-room blue on cool white. Cards carry a small pressure-isobar SVG motif in their corners; everything sits on a strict grid like an approach plate. The first screen shows a header band with the tagline, an inline search input, and the top row of briefing cards each led by a mono METAR capsule.
examples.signature: raw METAR strings render in bordered mono capsule blocks with a decoded plain-English line directly beneath

content.theme: meteorology briefings for private pilots decoding METARs, TAFs, and frontal systems

examples.name: nora
examples.title: Nora
examples.description: Oral histories, field recordings, and environmental sounds from the remote communities of the subarctic circle
examples.brief: A quiet, editorial audio journal documenting subarctic voices. The homepage bands are: (1) A clean header band containing a text-only title and a navigation menu; (2) A hero band with a two-column layout showing the podcast description on the left and a static, stylized SVG mountain range silhouette on the right; (3) A vertical timeline band where episodes stack chronologically, each episode card showing the season number, release date, title, a play button, and a mini-map SVG; (4) A minimal footer band with directory links and a theme toggle. Light mode uses a pale frost-tinted paper background and an ocean-cyan accent; dark mode shifts to deep fjord-blue and a desaturated cyan accent.
examples.signature: the vertical timeline spine is a dotted line of latitude numbers, and hovering an episode card highlights its corresponding node on the spine and displays its local recording temperature in a small badge

content.theme: field recordings and oral history dispatches from subarctic fishing villages, weather stations, and research camps

examples.name: notch
examples.title: Notch
examples.description: Version history for a chiptune tracker with FM synthesis channels
examples.brief: Release notes styled after the chiptune tracker itself: dark plum ground, hot-magenta accent, DM Mono everywhere, faint row-grid textures echoing a pattern editor. The first screen shows the current version inside a tracker-style header grid with channel columns, the latest changes listed beneath in tight mono rows with hex-flavored row numbers.
examples.signature: version numbers render inside a tracker pattern-editor header grid, with release channels laid out as labeled track columns

content.theme: release notes for a chiptune music tracker with FM synthesis channels

examples.name: ochre
examples.title: Ochre
examples.description: Title sequences and kinetic idents from a motion studio obsessed with easing curves
examples.brief: A motion design studio for film title sequences and broadcast idents, presented as a dark studio wall of project tiles. Warm gold accents on near-black, Space Grotesk headings, and tile chrome that borrows from timeline UIs: in/out points, frame counts, duration stamps. The first screen shows the studio name, a one-line reel description, and the top of a bento grid where each tile carries its project's easing curve drawn as an SVG path.
examples.signature: each project tile carries its own cubic-bezier easing curve as an SVG path that animates a dot along itself on hover

content.theme: film title sequences and kinetic broadcast idents, broken down beat by beat

examples.name: ohm
examples.title: Ohm
examples.description: Cookieless web analytics with every number computed on European soil
examples.brief: A privacy-first analytics company that proves its restraint through the design itself: warm off-white ground, one teal accent, generous whitespace, zero decorative charts. The homepage opens with a plain-language promise headline over a bento grid of metric tiles, plus a two-line install snippet in a code block.
examples.signature: hero bento tiles show metrics as bare oversized tabular numerals with one-word labels and no chart lines anywhere

content.theme: cookieless web analytics hosted entirely in the EU

examples.name: olivine
examples.title: Olivine
examples.description: Build streaming ETL pipelines with backpressure that just works
examples.brief: Data-pipeline docs with a botanical calm unusual for infrastructure: warm green-tinted neutrals, leaf-green accent, Hanken Grotesk at friendly weights, softly rounded code surfaces. Pipelines are drawn as growing structures rather than boxes and arrows. The homepage first screen shows the tagline beside a curved SVG vine connecting three stage nodes — source, transform, sink.
examples.signature: pipeline stages appear as leaf-shaped SVG nodes joined by a curved vine that runs down the page margin

content.theme: a streaming ETL framework with native backpressure and exactly-once sinks

examples.name: orbit
examples.title: Orbit
examples.description: A human-curated search engine that indexes only the independent web
examples.brief: An AI-free search engine landing with early-web directory charm rebuilt on modern craft: deep aubergine-black ground, lavender accent, Zilla Slab headings that nod to the old open web. A header band holds the pitch and a mock search field; below it, a card grid of hand-curated directory categories (personal sites, webrings, tiny tools), each card counting its indexed sites. First screen: pitch, search field, and the top category row.
examples.signature: a hand-lettered-style SVG starburst badge reading NO MODELS, JUST PEOPLE sits rotated twelve degrees beside the hero search field

content.theme: a search engine whose index is built entirely by human curators and excludes machine-generated pages

examples.name: pascal
examples.title: Pascal
examples.description: Risograph and letterpress printing for short runs and stubborn ideas
examples.brief: A print shop landing built like a press sheet before trimming: newsprint-toned ground, harsh black rules, Archivo in heavy weights, and one riso-red accent. Blocks sit slightly rotated and overlapped, zine-collage style, with exposed borders and raw uppercase labels. First screen: the shop name stamped huge, a price-per-run table fragment, and a rotated 'NOW BOOKING SPRING RUNS' slab.
examples.signature: CMYK registration marks and crop ticks frame every major block, as if the page were an untrimmed press sheet

content.theme: a risograph and letterpress print shop for short runs

examples.name: pat
examples.title: Pat
examples.description: CV of a patent agent specializing in cryptography and hardware security patents
examples.brief: A patent agent's CV styled as an official patent publication: a dense two-column broadsheet layout on cream-aged paper, shifting to blueprints-blue in dark mode via the theme toggle. Indigo-navy accent color highlights drawing numbers and classification labels; meta lines use patent claim formatting. The first screen presents a bold masthead block styled after a patent title page, complete with a custom stamp mark, an abstract section, and a timeline index of patent filings that lists each work history entry as a published utility patent.
examples.signature: each work history entry has a detailed SVG schematic drawing with labeled reference numerals that highlight on hover

content.theme: CV of a patent agent specializing in cryptography and hardware security patents

examples.name: penumbra
examples.title: Penumbra
examples.description: An after-dark modular synth meetup of patch sessions and ambient sets
examples.brief: A late-night synth meetup styled like the front panel of a modular rack: deep charcoal, hairline geometric borders, one hot magenta. The homepage is a bento grid — a large tile with the meetup name and date (Friday 18 September 2026, Rotterdam), medium tiles for each performance slot, small tiles for venue and door time. Tiles feel like modules: labeled corners, small circular jack marks, tight uppercase micro-labels.
examples.signature: thin magenta SVG patch-cable curves are drawn between the corners of adjacent bento tiles, wiring the schedule together like a rack

content.theme: a fictional monthly modular synthesizer meetup with live patch sessions, gear swaps, and ambient closing sets

examples.name: perigee
examples.title: Perigee
examples.description: A 38mm hand-wound moonphase watch, made in numbered runs of 300
examples.brief: A mechanical watch microbrand presented like a night-sky vitrine: near-black warm ground, champagne-gold accent, Playfair Display for the wordmark and section titles. Full-bleed bands alternate between the watch story, movement details, and the journal teaser, each with a single centered SVG illustration. First screen: the wordmark over a large moonphase dial, run number '138 / 300' set small beneath.
examples.signature: a large SVG moonphase dial sits behind the hero wordmark with exactly one lune picked out in the accent gold

content.theme: a hand-wound moonphase watch made in numbered runs of 300

examples.name: perihelion
examples.title: Perihelion
examples.description: A January bread festival celebrating long ferments, hot ovens, and cold mornings
examples.brief: A winter bread festival that feels like a warm bakery window on a frozen street: flour-white background, crust-brown accent, round friendly display type. The homepage leads with the festival name and dates (24–25 January 2026, a market hall in Ghent) above a vertical day schedule — bake-offs, oven firings, and tasting flights hanging off a central spine. Warm, generous, and a little floury; decoration comes from typographic ornaments, not photos.
examples.signature: schedule times sit inside oval proofing-basket badges ringed with concentric border lines, like the spiral of a banneton

content.theme: a fictional artisan bread festival with sourdough bake-offs, wood-oven firings, and grain-miller talks

examples.name: perseid
examples.title: Perseid
examples.description: An August star party timed to the meteor shower, with all-night observing sessions
examples.brief: A star party site that reads like a well-set almanac page at night: deep blue-black sky, ivory serif type, a single dim-gold accent tuned for dark adaptation. The homepage opens with the party name and dates (11–13 August 2026, a dark-sky reserve in the Brecon Beacons) as a small classical masthead, then drops straight into the night-by-night observing timeline. Ornament is limited to fine rules and star glyphs; everything stays quiet enough to protect night vision.
examples.signature: the observing timeline is drawn as a star chart — each session hangs from a plotted gold star dot, with faint constellation lines connecting consecutive slots

content.theme: a fictional dark-sky star party scheduled around the Perseid meteor shower peak, with telescope clinics and all-night watches

examples.name: petrichor
examples.title: Petrichor
examples.description: A rain-garden diary tracking what survives heavy clay and heavier weather
examples.brief: A gardener's diary about coaxing a rain garden out of heavy clay soil. Soft moss-and-cream palette, Lora headings, rounded surfaces, and gentle botanical warmth without kitsch. The homepage's first screen shows the site title over a one-paragraph introduction to the plot, an inline search input, and the top of the planting diary list.
examples.signature: section headings are underlined with a hand-drawn SVG root-line squiggle instead of a straight rule

content.theme: a rain-garden diary tracking what thrives in heavy clay soil

examples.name: postmark
examples.title: Postmark
examples.description: A weekly broadsheet digest of browser releases, platform flags, and spec movements
examples.brief: A weekly web-platform digest laid out as a dark broadsheet: masthead in condensed caps, hairline column rules, small-caps section labels, dense but disciplined. Ink-dark ground with a single cyan accent reserved for links, issue numbers, and the subscribe strip. The first screen shows the masthead over the lead issue's two-column standfirst, a rule-separated well of recent issues, and a static subscribe form pinned as a bottom band.
examples.signature: an SVG postage-meter cancellation mark stamps the lead issue's dateline in the masthead

content.theme: a weekly digest of web-platform releases, browser flags, and spec movements

examples.name: postscript
examples.title: Postscript
examples.description: Personal finance for working artists: irregular income, grants, and ledgers
examples.brief: A personal-finance letter for working artists that borrows the broadsheet's discipline and then loosens its tie: cream newsprint ground, burnt-orange accent, kickers set in exuberant Fraunces. Money talk stays warm — figures are big, jargon is footnoted, rules are hand-thick. The first screen shows the masthead, a lead issue with an oversized headline figure, two columns of recent issues, and a subscribe coupon boxed like a classified ad.
examples.signature: each issue's key figure renders as an oversized old-style numeral inside a ruled ledger box

content.theme: personal finance for working artists: irregular income, grants, and studio bookkeeping

examples.name: prisma
examples.title: Prisma
examples.description: Spectral studies of light passing through cut glass, painted with SVG gradients
examples.brief: A gradient-study gallery on the optics of cut glass: every plate is an SVG composition of refracted spectral bands, caustic fans, and dispersion wedges built from SVG linearGradients, never CSS gradients. The page itself stays cool and clinical — pale violet-white ground, tight grotesque type — so each plate reads like a specimen under lab light. The homepage first screen shows the site name split through an SVG prism in the masthead above a masonry wall of spectrum plates.
examples.signature: the masthead splits the site name through an SVG prism, fanning thin spectral bars out behind the letters

content.theme: spectral refraction studies of light passing through cut glass

examples.name: propel
examples.title: Propel
examples.description: A keyboard-driven email client built to reach inbox zero in minutes a day
examples.brief: An email client landing arranged as a bento grid of capability tiles: triage shortcuts, snooze, split inboxes, send-later. Manrope everywhere, cool blue accent, crisp 1px borders. The first screen leads with a large tile containing a mock inbox column of three plausible emails, surrounded by smaller stat and shortcut tiles. Ships a light/dark toggle that echoes the client's own day/night reading modes.
examples.signature: every feature tile carries its keyboard shortcut as a rendered keycap chip in the top-right corner

content.theme: a keyboard-first email client whose triage flow archives, snoozes, or replies in one keystroke

examples.name: pumice
examples.title: Pumice
examples.description: Deploy a telemetry agent that costs less than the metrics it ships
examples.brief: Observability-agent docs styled as a refined ops console: slate-black ground, cool sky-blue accent, Inter Tight headings, panel regions separated by hairline borders rather than heavy chrome. Data density with poise — tables, metric names, and units are typeset with care. The homepage first screen is the app-shell topbar with its sparkline strip above panels for quickstart, collectors, and the latest release.
examples.signature: the topbar embeds a live-looking metrics strip of tiny inline SVG sparklines labeled in mono

content.theme: a lightweight eBPF-based telemetry agent with adaptive sampling

examples.name: quartzite
examples.title: Quartzite
examples.description: A payments SDK reference covering subscriptions, invoicing, and idempotent charges
examples.brief: A refined, banking-grade docs site for a payments SDK: cool porcelain background, precise Instrument Sans, and a single confident blue for links and buttons. The homepage first screen offers a restrained masthead, one sentence of promise, and a ruled index of API guides with route names in mono. Whitespace and hairline double rules carry the sense of trust.
examples.signature: every API page opens with a ledger-rule header: mono route on the left, method badge on the right, double hairline beneath

content.theme: docs for Quartzite, a payments SDK for subscriptions, invoicing, and idempotent charge handling

examples.name: quire
examples.title: Quire
examples.description: A practical handbook of typographic craft for the web-reading age
examples.brief: A practical typography handbook that practices what it preaches: warm paper, disciplined Spectral headings, a crisp red accent used only for links and rules, and a chapter sidebar that doubles as the table of contents. The homepage first screen shows the handbook title at hero scale over a one-line thesis and the numbered chapter list. Every chapter ends with prev/next links driven by page.lower and page.higher.
examples.signature: type specimens sit in ruled specimen boxes labeled with pica measurements along the top edge

content.theme: a practical handbook of typographic craft: measure, rhythm, hierarchy, and restraint for screen reading

examples.name: raku
examples.title: Raku
examples.description: Coastal raku tea ware, fired in salt air and documented one kiln opening at a time
examples.brief: A ceramicist who fires raku tea bowls in a coastal kiln, where the salt air unpredictably marks each glaze. Quiet stone-white pages, generous whitespace, serif headings with wide tracking on small-caps labels. The homepage splits: a short manifesto on the left, and on the right a vertical index of the latest firings, each entry paired with a single hairline SVG contour of the vessel's profile.
examples.signature: every vessel is drawn as a single hairline SVG profile contour beside its case study, no other imagery

content.theme: raku-fired tea ware from a coastal kiln, documented firing by firing

examples.name: resonance
examples.title: Resonance
examples.description: Rooftop plots, compost math, and community growing — an urban farming podcast
examples.brief: An urban-farming podcast that feels sunlit and hand-tended. Warm green-white ground, leaf-green accent, Fredoka headings, and rounded episode cards in a friendly grid. First screen: the masthead, a one-line growing-season note, and the card grid with garden-mound waveforms rolling under each episode title.
examples.signature: the waveform SVG under each episode title is drawn as a row of garden-bed mounds, peaks scaled to the audio's loudness curve

content.theme: an urban-farming podcast on rooftop plots and community growing

examples.name: reverb
examples.title: Reverb
examples.description: A cryptography podcast decoding ciphers from Caesar wheels to lattice schemes
examples.brief: A cryptography podcast with a codebook aesthetic. Ink-violet dark ground, lavender accent, Fragment Mono throughout, episode cards gridded like cipher blocks with letter-spaced headers. First screen: the masthead set in wide-tracked mono above the grid, each card's show-note preview sitting ROT13-scrambled until decoded on hover.
examples.signature: show-note previews render ROT13-scrambled on each episode card and decrypt in place on hover or focus

content.theme: a cryptography podcast decoding ciphers from Caesar to lattice schemes

examples.name: revue
examples.title: Revue
examples.description: Essays on restored classics and the repertory screens that keep them lit
examples.brief: A cinema periodical dressed for the dark: plum-black house lights, gilded accent, high-contrast serif display over hushed sans body. Essays carry a program kicker (RESTORATION / MIDNIGHT SLOT), a dek from the description, and the essayist's byline in letterspaced caps. The homepage first screen sets the Revue nameplate above a letterboxed lead feature and a two-column well of this month's program notes.
examples.signature: feature titles sit inside a 2.39:1 letterboxed band, black bars included, like a frame of projected film

content.theme: essays on restored classics and repertory cinema programming

examples.name: rhyolite
examples.title: Rhyolite
examples.description: Learn GPU-accelerated 2D vector rendering, from paths to compute shaders
examples.brief: Graphics-library docs with a night-lab blueprint mood: violet-black ground, hot pink accent, Sora headings with wide tracking, figures drawn as luminous line art. Learning pages alternate prose, code, and diagram in a steady rhythm. The homepage first screen shows the library name over a large inline SVG of bezier control cages rendered in accent pink on the blueprint grid.
examples.signature: every figure is an inline SVG plotted on a faint blueprint grid that bleeds past the content column edges

content.theme: a GPU-accelerated 2D vector graphics library with a retained scene graph

examples.name: rime
examples.title: Rime
examples.description: Spare dispatches from alpine ascents above the frost line
examples.brief: An alpine climbing log in charcoal blue-black with ultra-condensed Big Shoulders display type and ice-blue accents, stark as a north face. The homepage hero splits: the latest ascent's title and grade on the left, and behind-right, its summit elevation set at enormous scale in ghosted condensed numerals. Interior pages are terse, gear-listed, and unsentimental.
examples.signature: each ascent's summit elevation sits at hero scale in ghosted ultra-condensed numerals behind the post title

content.theme: alpine climbing dispatches from high granite and mixed-ice routes

examples.name: rowan
examples.title: Rowan
examples.description: A world-building codex for the fractured archipelago empire of Veyra
examples.brief: A fantasy world-building wiki that reads like a scholar's annotated codex: warm ivory paper, wine-dark accents, small-caps rubric labels over each sidebar nav group, and hairline double rules under article titles. The homepage first screen shows the codex masthead, a one-paragraph gazetteer introduction, and the sidebar tree of regions and houses already open. Interior articles keep long scholarly prose with cross-reference links set in the accent wine.
examples.signature: each article's infobox floats right as a parchment-toned ledger card with ruled rows for ruler, banner, and founding year

content.theme: a shared-world atlas wiki for Veyra, a fractured archipelago empire of rival trading houses

examples.name: saebyeok
examples.title: Saebyeok
examples.description: Essays written in the quiet hour before sunrise, set in candlelit garamond
examples.brief: A hushed pre-dawn writing journal named for the Korean word for dawn. Deep indigo-charcoal night palette warmed by a single rose-gold accent, wide margins, and unhurried garamond prose. The homepage opens on the latest essay's title alone at generous scale, with the entry list receding below in small muted type — the whole first screen should feel like a room lit by one lamp.
examples.signature: each essay replaces its category label with the exact pre-dawn time it was written, e.g. 05:12, set in small caps beside the date

content.theme: personal essays drafted in the quiet hour before sunrise

examples.name: salon
examples.title: Salon
examples.description: Photography criticism with no photographs — the prints live in the prose
examples.brief: A text-first photography criticism journal set like a darkroom reading room: charcoal-blue ground, pale silver-blue accent, one austere serif voice and enormous whitespace. Essays open with a kicker naming the medium (PHOTOBOOK / GELATIN SILVER), a dek from the description, and the critic's byline; no images anywhere, by principle. The homepage first screen shows the Salon wordmark, the lead essay's title at hero scale, and an empty outlined frame standing in for the unshown print.
examples.signature: essays open with an empty 3:2 frame outlined in hairline rule where the unshown photograph would sit

content.theme: text-only criticism of photobooks and exhibition prints

examples.name: schist
examples.title: Schist
examples.description: Rule catalog and pipeline guides for a multi-language lint runner
examples.brief: Linter docs with a raw, inspection-report attitude: stark white ground, thick black borders, olive-green accent, Archivo set heavy and tight. Rule pages read like citations, with the violating and fixed code shown side by side in bordered blocks. The homepage first screen stacks the tool name in massive caps over a bordered inline search input and the first rows of the rule index.
examples.signature: every rule page leads with a thick-bordered, slightly rotated severity stamp reading ERROR, WARN, or HINT

content.theme: a multi-language lint runner with shareable rule sets and autofix pipelines

examples.name: semaphore
examples.title: Semaphore

The homepage first screen shows the latest release as a live prompt session: the semver as the shell prompt, change lines printing beneath as command output.
examples.signature: releases render as prompt sessions — the semver is the shell prompt and change lists print as indented command output

content.theme: release notes for a GPU-accelerated terminal emulator with ligature support and split panes

examples.name: sepia
examples.title: Sepia
examples.description: A documentary photographer's project index, kept as annotated contact sheets

Deep warm black like an unexposed sheet, cream text, and a strict grid of empty film frames carrying only rebate edge markings, frame numbers, and captions. The first screen shows the photographer's name as a small header stamp above the contact-sheet grid of current projects, one frame circled in magenta grease pencil.
examples.signature: grid frames carry film-rebate edge markings and frame numbers, with a magenta grease-pencil SVG circle around the featured frame

content.theme: long-form documentary photography projects on working harbor towns, described frame by frame

examples.name: serein
examples.title: Serein
examples.description: Letterpress printing dispatches from a one-press basement shop
examples.brief: A letterpress printer's blog set as a broadsheet front page: masthead with hairline double rules, small-caps section kickers, column rules, and justified text on soft antique paper with a crimson ink accent. examples.signature: headlines are letterpress-debossed with layered text-shadow, deepening slightly on hover like a second impression

content.theme: running a one-press basement letterpress shop with a restored proofing press

examples.name: sienna
examples.title: Sienna

The homepage splits asymmetrically: practice statement and commission list on the left, a large layered SVG topographic contour drawing on the right with spot elevations labeled in the margin.
examples.signature: site plans render as layered SVG topographic contours with spot elevations labeled in the page margin

content.theme: post-industrial sites — rail yards, quarries, gasworks — redesigned as public parks

examples.name: sigil

examples.signature: the header renders as a shell prompt whose command history doubles as the site nav

examples.signature: pull-stat callouts hang in the outer margin like annotated proofs, each with a small source line
